events.title: CIPS Seminar: “Exoplanets in Galactic Space and Time, from Kepler and K2 to Roman”
events.description: Christopher Lam (Caltech) presents on exoplanets, covering insights from Kepler, K2, and Roman missions regarding their distribution in galactic space and time. This academic seminar is open to the public.

events.title: Zoom Botany Talk: Botanical Explorations in Namaqualand
events.description: Join Garden staff for a virtual plant expedition to Namaqualand, South Africa, a region known for its unique and fragile botanical diversity. Discover fascinating plant species and conservation efforts.

events.title: Reporting on and Researching the Politics of Food and Farming
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Graduate School of Journalism
events.description: Journalist Mark Schapiro engages in conversation with 'Food Fix' founder Helena Bottemiller Evich on critical issues surrounding the politics and research of food and farming.

events.title: Berkeley Book Chat with Andreja Novakovic
events.description: Participate in a stimulating book discussion with Andreja Novakovic, offering insights and perspectives on a featured literary work. This event fosters intellectual exchange within the campus community.

events.title: Inka Gastropolitics: stratified and stable
events.description: Explore the historical and cultural significance of Inka gastropolitics in this academic lecture. Delve into how food systems reflected and maintained social structures.

events.title: Indigenous Research Methods Speaker Series: The Scale of Indigenous Peoples' Actions to Address Climate Change
events.description: This speaker series highlights indigenous research methodologies and their application to understanding and addressing climate change. Learn about community-led initiatives and global impacts.

events.title: Foerster Lecture with Mark Johnston: Is This Your Only Life?
events.description: Mark Johnston delivers a thought-provoking lecture as part of the esteemed Foerster Lecture series, inviting reflection on existential questions and personal meaning.

events.title: AI & Society: AI and Democracy with Larry Norden, David Evan Harris, and Ashish Goel
events.description: A panel discussion exploring the complex intersection of artificial intelligence and democratic processes, featuring leading experts in the field.

events.title: Renee Y. Chow: A Call to Architects | Architecture Lecture
events.description: Architect Renee Y. Chow delivers a compelling lecture, offering her perspectives and vision for the future of architectural practice and design.

events.title: Environmental and Energy Economics Seminar: Tatyana Deryugina
events.description: Tatyana Deryugina from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign presents on environmental and energy economics. This seminar is a collaboration between Haas and other UC Berkeley departments.

events.title: CS Seminar: Workload-Aware Networks for Machine Learning
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences (EECS)
events.description: A computer science seminar exploring advanced topics in workload-aware networks, focusing on their applications and implications for machine learning systems.

events.title: Dissertation Talk: Privacy and Adaptivity Considerations in Modern Data Analysis
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences (EECS)
events.description: A graduate dissertation presentation on critical issues of privacy and adaptivity within contemporary data analysis methodologies and systems.

events.title: Berkeley Art, Law, and Finance Project: Justice Unearthed—Tim Carpenter on The Grave Robber
events.organizer: Berkeley Center for Law and Business
events.description: Join former FBI Lead Investigator Tim Carpenter in a discussion on 'The Grave Robber', exploring the intersections of art, law, and finance in cultural property cases.

events.title: Writing Complex Information into the Brain
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences (EECS)
events.description: A seminar exploring theories and methods related to the effective processing and encoding of complex information within the human brain.

events.title: Dissertation Talk: Understanding Misalignment in AI Agents
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences (EECS)
events.description: A graduate dissertation presentation analyzing the crucial problem of misalignment in artificial intelligence agents and potential solutions.

events.title: Seminar 211, Economic History: Mini-Conference
events.description: A day-long mini-conference featuring presentations from leading scholars on various topics in economic history, offering diverse research perspectives.

events.title: PE Seminar - Martin Castillo Quintana (U Chicago)
events.description: Martin Castillo Quintana from the University of Chicago delivers a seminar on a political economy topic, fostering discussion and academic exchange.

events.title: Physics Colloquium with Tongyan Lin
events.description: Tongyan Lin presents a physics colloquium, discussing current research and theoretical advancements in the field. This event is free and open to the public.

events.title: Berkeley Water Day: Managing Water Under Climate Extremes
events.description: A day dedicated to exploring innovative strategies and research for managing water resources in the face of escalating climate extremes.

events.title: CRELS Speaker Series: "Algorithms of Distinction: Class, Credit Scores, and Property in South Africa"
events.description: Julien Migozzi presents research on how algorithms are used to create social distinctions, focusing on class, credit scores, and property in South Africa.

events.title: The Annual Baxter Lecture: AI, Law, and Free Speech (Speaker: Eugene Volokh)
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Political Science / Berkeley Liberty Initiative
events.location: Banatao Auditorium, University of California, Berkeley
events.description: Distinguished speaker Eugene Volokh discusses the critical legal and societal implications at the intersection of artificial intelligence and free speech.

events.title: Seminar 242 Econometrics: “Can language models boost the power of randomized experiments without statistical bias?”
events.description: Xinwei Ma explores how language models can enhance the statistical power of randomized experiments while maintaining impartiality.

events.title: POWER Conference on Energy Research and Policy
events.location: Spieker Forum (6th floor of Chou Hall), Haas School of Business, UC Berkeley
events.description: An annual conference bringing together scholars, policymakers, and industry professionals to discuss cutting-edge research on energy markets and regulation.

events.title: Alone in the Algorithm: Human Connection in the Age of AI
events.description: A crucial discussion on the challenges and opportunities for maintaining human connection and community in an increasingly algorithm-driven and AI-centric world.

events.title: The Herb Caen Lecture: 'Press Freedom in Peril'
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Graduate School of Journalism
events.location: North Gate Hall Logan Multimedia Center at UC Berkeley
events.description: Jodie Ginsberg and Jason Rezaian discuss global threats to press freedom, moderated by Emilio Garcia-Ruiz. A reception precedes the conversation at 5:30 PM.

events.title: 2026 David E. Nelson Memorial Lecture
events.organizer: Berkeley Center for Law and Technology (BCLT)
events.description: A seminar exploring the intersection of business and law, focusing on how competition and innovation policy might evolve with greater cross-pollination between these fields.

events.title: The Inaugural UC Berkeley School of Public Health John Swartzberg Lecture
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.location: UC Berkeley School of Public Health (implied)
events.description: Dr. Erica Pan, Director and State Public Health Officer for the California Department of Public Health, delivers the inaugural John Swartzberg Annual Lecture.

events.title: 2026 BioEHSC, A Bioengineering High School Competition
events.description: A competition for high school students in bioengineering, showcasing innovative projects and fostering interest in the field.

events.title: Physics Colloquium with Markus Greiner
events.description: Markus Greiner, from Harvard University, presents a physics colloquium, sharing his latest research and insights. The event is open to the public.

events.title: Authors Meet Critics: 'The Insatiable Machine: How Capitalism Conquered the World' (Trevor Jackson)
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Political Science
events.description: A panel discussion and critique of Trevor Jackson's book, 'The Insatiable Machine', exploring historical perspectives on capitalism's global expansion.

events.title: Dr. Rocco Friebel: Private Equity in Healthcare
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: Dr. Rocco Friebel delivers a presentation on the growing influence and implications of private equity investments within the healthcare sector.

events.title: 2026 Berkeley Corporate + Climate Summit
events.organizer: Berkeley Center for Law and Business
events.location: Spieker Forum at Berkeley Haas – Chou Hall
events.description: A summit dedicated to exploring critical issues at the intersection of corporate governance, business strategy, and climate change initiatives.

events.title: Authors Meet Critics: Incommunicable: Toward Communicative Justice in Health and Medicine
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Anthropology Department / Public Health
events.description: Panel discussion on Charles Briggs' book, delving into concepts of communicative justice within health and medicine, with insights from various scholars.

events.title: 2026 Wildavsky Lecture: Jens Ludwig
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Political Science
events.description: Jens Ludwig, author of 'Unforgiving Places: The Unexpected Origins of American Gun Violence', delivers the annual Wildavsky Lecture.

events.title: 11th Annual BayViro Symposium
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: The 11th annual symposium bringing together researchers and practitioners to discuss the latest advancements and challenges in environmental health and virology.

events.title: Physics Colloquium with Luca Iliesiu
events.description: Luca Iliesiu presents on cutting-edge topics in physics research as part of the department's colloquium series. This event is open to the public.

events.title: BCLB Business Law Practice Area Workshop: The Digital Deal – AI's Impact on Real Estate Transactions with Gibson Dunn
events.organizer: Berkeley Center for Law and Business
events.description: A workshop exploring the transformative effects of artificial intelligence on the legal aspects and practices within real estate transactions.

events.title: Beyond the Bench: How Science Becomes Medicine – and a Career.
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: Discover the journey from scientific discovery to medical application and explore diverse career paths in translational science and health.

events.title: Authors Meet Critics: 'Demos: How the People Ruled Athens' (Daniela Cammack)
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Political Science
events.description: A panel convenes to discuss Daniela Cammack's forthcoming book on the mechanics of popular rule in ancient Athens and its modern relevance.

events.title: Energy Markets Workshop – Omer Karaduman, Stanford University
events.location: Haas School of Business, UC Berkeley (implied)
events.description: Omer Karaduman from Stanford University leads a workshop on current trends and research in energy markets.

events.title: CRELS speaker series with Simone Zhang: "Scoring Justice: Risk Assessment Tools, Court Practices, and Fairness Perceptions"
events.description: Simone Zhang explores the fairness and societal impact of risk assessment tools used in legal and judicial systems.

events.title: Energy Markets Workshop – Aaron Smith, UC Berkeley
events.location: Haas School of Business, UC Berkeley (implied)
events.description: Aaron Smith from UC Berkeley leads a workshop on advanced topics and emerging research in energy markets.

events.title: Spring 2026 Applied AI Bootcamp for Lawyers
events.description: An intensive bootcamp designed for legal professionals to understand and apply artificial intelligence technologies in legal practice.

events.title: Curators' Tour: Margot Norton and Tausif Noor on Rhapsody: Works from the Cooper Rosenwasser Collection
events.description: Chief Curator Margot Norton and Curatorial Associate Tausif Noor discuss selected works from the Cooper Rosenwasser Collection, offering expert insights.

events.title: Film Screening – “Far from Home”
events.description: A screening of the film 'Far from Home', with an introduction provided by UC Berkeley Professor Minoo Moallem.

events.title: Noon Concert: Chamber Music
events.description: Enjoy a free midday concert featuring chamber music performances by talented student musicians from the UC Berkeley Department of Music.

events.title: Exhibit Opening Reception: The Etruscans Uncovered
events.organizer: Phoebe A. Hearst Museum of Anthropology / The Bancroft Library
events.location: Doe Library's Bernice Layne Brown Gallery, UC Berkeley
events.description: Celebrate the opening of 'The Etruscans Uncovered' exhibit, showcasing a selection from the Phoebe A. Hearst Museum of Anthropology's collection.

events.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: Experience a captivating performance of Shakespeare's classic comedy, 'A Midsummer Night's Dream', presented by a UC Berkeley department.

events.title: UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra Concert
events.description: The UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra performs a selection of classical and contemporary works in this evening concert.

events.title: UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra Concert
events.description: Another evening performance by the UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra, showcasing their talent and diverse repertoire.

events.title: A Conversation with Actor and Producer Poorna Jagannathan
events.description: Join an insightful conversation with renowned actor and producer Poorna Jagannathan, discussing her career and experiences in the entertainment industry.

events.title: UC Berkeley Chamber Chorus and University Chorus featuring UCBSO - Verdi's Requiem
events.description: A magnificent performance of Verdi's Requiem by the UC Berkeley Chamber Chorus, University Chorus, and Symphony Orchestra.

events.title: Taceti Ensemble. Public Concert
events.description: The Taceti Ensemble presents a public concert featuring innovative new musical works by various composers.

events.title: Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater
events.description: The acclaimed Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater performs a powerful and moving repertoire of contemporary dance.

events.title: Film Screening – Legally Blonde
events.description: Enjoy a screening of the popular film 'Legally Blonde', a fun event for UC Berkeley students.

events.title: Cal Performances' 2026 Gala: The Joffrey Ballet – Midsummer Night's Dream
events.description: An elegant gala evening featuring a performance by The Joffrey Ballet of 'Midsummer Night's Dream', supporting Cal Performances' artistic initiatives.

events.title: Jordi Savall; La Capella Reial de Catalunya and Hespèrion XXI
events.description: Master musician Jordi Savall leads La Capella Reial de Catalunya and Hespèrion XXI in a concert of historical music.

events.title: The Tallis Scholars: Mysteries and Miracles
events.description: The renowned Tallis Scholars perform an Easter season program of choral works inspired by biblical narratives.

events.title: Víkingur Ólafsson, piano: Opus 109
events.description: Artist in Residence pianist Víkingur Ólafsson performs a recital featuring Beethoven's Piano Sonata Op. 109 and other works.

events.title: Berkeley Dance Project 2026
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: Showcasing the talent of UC Berkeley's dance students and choreographers in an exciting annual performance.

events.title: Cal Bears Baseball vs. North Carolina Tar Heels
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Come support the Cal Bears baseball team as they face the North Carolina Tar Heels in an exciting home game.

events.title: Women's Gymnastics 2026 Cal Tri Meet
events.description: Witness high-flying routines and precision as the Cal Women's Gymnastics team competes in a home tri-meet.

events.title: Cal Bears Baseball vs. North Carolina Tar Heels
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Another opportunity to cheer on the Cal Bears baseball team in their home series against the North Carolina Tar Heels.

events.title: Cal Bears Baseball vs. North Carolina Tar Heels
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The final game of the home series for Cal Bears Baseball against the North Carolina Tar Heels.

events.title: Women's Gymnastics 2026 Cal Quad Meet
events.description: The Cal Women's Gymnastics squad hosts a quad meet, showcasing their skills against multiple teams.

events.title: Cal Bears Baseball vs. UConn
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Catch the Cal Bears baseball team in action as they compete against UConn in a home game at Stu Gordon Stadium.

events.title: Intro to Pickleball Clinic
events.description: A beginner-friendly clinic to learn the basic rules, court positioning, and fundamental shots of pickleball in a small group setting.

events.title: Cal Bears Baseball vs. Saint Mary's
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The Cal Bears baseball team plays a home game against local rivals Saint Mary's.

events.title: Cal Bears Baseball vs. Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets
events.location: Evans Diamond At Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: Don't miss the Cal Bears baseball team taking on the Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets in a competitive home game.

events.title: Re-Entry and Transfer Student Event
events.description: A welcoming social gathering exclusively for re-entry and transfer students, offering refreshments, snacks, and a chance to connect.

events.title: Dreaming Circle: visionary fiction as abolitionist practice
events.description: Join a dreaming circle dedicated to exploring visionary fiction as a tool for abolitionist thought and practice.

events.title: The Loft Hour: Tadiwa Madenga + Juliana Ramírez Herrera
events.description: An engaging 'Loft Hour' event featuring presentations or performances by Tadiwa Madenga and Juliana Ramírez Herrera.

events.title: CrafterDark – Beaded Potted Plant
events.organizer: Student Union at UC Berkeley / Cub Concerts at Berkeley
events.description: Unleash your creativity at this weekly craft session, making a beaded potted plant while enjoying live music.

events.title: Accel Scholars Info-Session
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences (EECS)
events.description: An informational session for students interested in learning more about the Accel Scholars program and its opportunities.

events.title: St. Patrick's Day Celebration
events.description: Celebrate St. Patrick's Day with festive crafts, sweet treats, and engaging activities hosted by the Student Union.

events.title: March Game Night!
events.organizer: Student Union at UC Berkeley / Board Games at Berkeley
events.description: Join Board Games at Berkeley for a fun evening of games, snacks, and socializing, marking the final game night of the spring semester.

events.title: Integral Taiji and Qigong
events.description: A serene and invigorating class focused on Integral Taiji and Qigong practices amidst the natural beauty of the Botanical Garden.

events.title: Grad Student Appreciation Patio Party
events.organizer: Graduate Social Club and the Grad Division (UC Berkeley)
events.description: A social gathering for graduate students to relax, network, and enjoy tasty bites in appreciation of their hard work.

events.title: International Founder Roadmap: F-1, CPT/OPT & Beyond
events.description: International Founder Roadmap: F-1, CPT/OPT & Beyond

events.title: DIY Shirt Patches
events.organizer: Student Union at UC Berkeley / Cub Concerts at Berkeley
events.description: A hands-on workshop where students can create their own custom shirt patches, fostering creativity and personal expression.

events.title: Youth & AI Innovation Summit 2026
events.organizer: BlueRobins AI Kiran CSUA, UC BerkeleyCALICO, UC Berkeley
events.description: A national summit celebrating youth innovation across AI, education, hardware, life sciences, sustainability, engineering, and deep tech, designed for students and educators.

events.title: Ecology Law Quarterly's 55th Anniversary Banquet
events.organizer: Ecology Law Quarterly, UC Berkeley Law
events.description: The annual banquet celebrating the 55th anniversary of the Ecology Law Quarterly, a highlight for the environmental law community.

events.title: Community Service Orgs Fair
events.description: Explore various community service organizations on campus and find opportunities to get involved and make a difference.

events.title: Drag Bingo
events.description: An entertaining evening of drag bingo, offering laughter, prizes, and a vibrant social experience for students.

events.title: Skydeck Accelerator Program Orientation
events.description: Orientation for startups selected for the Skydeck Accelerator Batch 22 Program, providing an introduction to resources and expectations.

events.title: Energy & Environmental Law Certificate Ceremony
events.location: Room 100 and Steinhart Courtyard, Berkeley Law
events.description: A ceremony celebrating the 2026 recipients of the Environmental and Energy & Clean Technology Law Certificates of Specialization.

events.title: Berkeley Deep Tech Innovation Matchmaking Summit
events.organizer: UC Berkeley's Deep Tech Innovation Lab
events.location: Berkeley (in person; location shared upon acceptance)
events.description: An exclusive summit connecting deep tech founders with graduate students and venture capitalists to foster innovation and talent.

events.title: Skydeck Incubator- UC Berkeley (Startup Boost and Networking Session)
events.description: An in-person event at Berkeley Skydeck designed to boost startups and facilitate networking among entrepreneurs and industry experts.

events.title: UC LAUNCH Spring 2026: LAUNCHATHON
events.description: A two-day hackathon weekend offering workshops and networking opportunities for students to collaborate with UC LAUNCH Startups.

events.title: Building a Better Bagel With Boichik Bagels Founder Emily Winston
events.description: Emily Winston, founder of Boichik Bagels, shares her entrepreneurial journey and insights into building a successful food business.

events.title: Global Venture Lab (GVL) Conference on AI in Entrepreneurship Education
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Sutardja Center for Entrepreneurship & Technology (SCET)
events.description: A conference exploring the profound impact of AI on entrepreneurship education, with discussions and workshops led by top AI experts.

events.title: Global Venture Lab (GVL) Conference on AI in Entrepreneurship Education (Day 2)
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Sutardja Center for Entrepreneurship & Technology (SCET)
events.description: The second day of the Global Venture Lab conference, continuing discussions and collaborative workshops on AI's role in shaping entrepreneurial learning.

events.title: UC LAUNCH Spring 2026: DEMO DAY
events.description: An opportunity to witness the next generation of entrepreneurs as UC LAUNCH startups present their innovative ideas and ventures.
